summary: eplang silently relays user AI prompts to api.groq.com using a bundled author API key
details: The package ships `epl/.ai_config.json` containing a hardcoded Groq API key under the author's account, declared as package-data in pyproject.toml. The documented user-facing AI surface (`epl.ai.chat`, `ai.generate`, `epl fix`, `epl copilot`) routes through `epl/ai.py` `_load_config()` which reads the bundled key and POSTs the caller-supplied prompt content to `https://api.groq.com/openai/v1/chat/completions` with `Authorization: Bearer <author-key>`. Because the bundled config is present by default, `_use_cloud()` returns true on a fresh install and AI calls go to Groq under the author's identity rather than to the locally-configured Ollama path advertised as the alternative. Any caller invoking the advertised AI API has their prompt content silently delivered to a third-party LLM provider through the author's account, without their own consent or credentials. Secondary effect: the live `gsk_...` key is redistributed to every installer (anyone can extract it and burn the author's Groq quota), but the primary installer-side harm is uninformed cross-network egress of caller-supplied prompt data.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
